title: Knicks celebrate 2026 championship with New York parade
summary: New York celebrated the Knicks' first championship since 1973 with a parade route through Lower Manhattan and City Hall speeches from Jalen Brunson, Mike Brown, and other team figures.

title: Sean Sweeney takes over as Magic head coach
summary: Orlando introduced Sean Sweeney after the Spurs' Finals run, with the new coach framing the Magic opening as the right basketball and organizational fit.

title: Tiago Splitter begins Bulls tenure with compete-first message
summary: Chicago's new head coach leaned on his playing and coaching background while laying out an early message that the Bulls expect to compete immediately.

title: Harden and LeBron headline early 2026 free-agency watch
summary: NBA.com's free-agent preview highlights James Harden, LeBron James, and other major offseason decision points as teams begin planning before signings can become official.

title: Report frames Darryn Peterson's Jazz hesitation around roster fit
summary: Bleacher Report, citing Jake Fischer, says Peterson's camp may have concerns about a Utah fit because of overlap with Keyonte George. This is tracked as a report, not a settled team or player statement.

title: Offseason injury trackers list several June 16 status updates
summary: The CBS Sports injury board shows multiple updated offseason designations from June 16. Basketball Savant treats these as status-feed items and cross-checks injury language against official reporting when game availability is involved.

title: Knicks-Spurs Finals deliver strongest viewership since 1998
summary: The 2026 NBA Finals averaged more than 20 million viewers on ABC and ESPN, making it the league's most-watched Finals series in 28 years.

title: NBA trims early-entry field before June 23-24 Draft
summary: The league announced three additional international withdrawals, leaving 26 college early-entry candidates and five international early-entry candidates in the 2026 Draft pool.

title: NBA and Grizzlies mourn Brandon Clarke
summary: NBA.com confirmed the passing of Memphis forward Brandon Clarke at 29. Commissioner Adam Silver, the Grizzlies, and the NBPA released statements remembering Clarke's impact.
